title: Ocimum pyramidatum
taxonomy: Ocimum pyramidatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Ocimum, which includes various types of basil and mint plants. The Lamiaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as mint or deadnettle family. This family contains over 7,000 species, including many popular herbs and ornamental plants. Ocimum pyramidatum is native to tropical regions of Africa.

intro: Ocimum pyramidatum, also known as pyramidal basil, is a perennial plant species within the Lamiaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate various health issues including fever, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Ocimum pyramidatum include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Ocimum pyramidatum is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin health and wound healing.
